vulnerability.reference: Non-stationary client participation leads to temporal drift in fairness weights, degrading prediction quality for underrepresented language groups.
vulnerability.generated: Lower-weighted demographic groups lack sufficient representation in model updates, so their text predictions are easily destabilized by optimization drift.

impact.reference: Minority language users receive inaccurate or offensive text suggestions, harming user trust and communication.
impact.generated: Users in lower-weighted groups receive erratic text suggestions, causing frustration, slower typing, and diminished trust in the predictive keyboard.

dose_response.reference: AAggFF's fairness optimization under non-stationary client participation causes temporal drift in group-wise performance, which in cross-device predictive text exposes smartphone users to unpredictable suggestions, leading to inaccurate or offensive text for minority language users.
dose_response.generated: AAggFF's temporal drift in group-wise performance under non-stationary client participation, through smartphone keyboard prediction across diverse demographics, triggers destabilized text predictions for lower-weighted groups lacking representation, leading to erratic suggestions, frustration, and diminished trust.
